title: `podman cp` glob support
body:
### Feature request description

`podman cp b08e33c92e95:/workspace/ws/*.json /tmp/somepath/` does not work while specifying individual files work correctly.

It will be nice to have a feature to support `globs` in `podman cp`

### Suggest potential solution

`podman cp` should support globs

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I just copied individul files

### Additional context

None

comments.body:
Do we want this? Does this not break docker cp compat? Practically speaking changing this would be a breaking change because glob chars are then interpreted instead of treated like normal path chars, i.e. any normal pathname can contain such chars.

The man page even highlights this is not supported and blame brings me to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11375 https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11346 https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11194

So I guess nothing really changed since then so I guess wontfix might be best answer here?

Thanks for the background on this. Also, from https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11346:
> We've tried globbing on cp before, and it resulted in more than one CVE before we dropped the idea.

I think we can safely close this, but leave that to @flouthoc if he has something beyond what's already shared.

@Luap99 I was not aware of the past issues thanks for linking those. I think we can close the issue if it can't be supported due to compatibility issues.

But definitely this would be a great addition since I found this while using it in one of the workflows, but I am not blocked on this.
